tasks.title: Monitor CPU Utilization
tasks.description: Use existing system tools to retrieve current CPU usage metrics and identify any anomalies or performance deviations.

solution.approach: Analyze the system to determine available tools for retrieving CPU utilization metrics. Identify whether standard command-line utilities such as 'top', 'htop', 'mpstat', or 'vmstat' are present and suitable for capturing current CPU usage without requiring custom script development.
solution.algorithm: Check availability of common CLI monitoring tools via 'which' or direct execution; evaluate output format and real-time capability.

solution.approach: Design a lightweight monitoring strategy that leverages existing system tools to fetch CPU utilization data. Prefer non-intrusive, short-lived commands that provide immediate feedback. Select 'mpstat' if available due to its precise per-CPU statistics; otherwise fall back to 'top' in batch mode or parse '/proc/stat'. Avoid writing new Python scripts unless absolutely necessary.
solution.algorithm: Use conditional logic: attempt 'mpstat -P ALL 1 1', then 'top -bn1 | grep "Cpu(s)"', finally parse '/proc/stat' as fallback.

solution.approach: Execute the selected CLI command(s) to retrieve current CPU utilization metrics. Capture stdout directly without redirection to files. Use shell command execution with proper error handling and timeout safeguards.

Validate retrieved CPU metrics by checking for anomalies such as consistently high idle time (indicating underutilization), zero usage across all cores, or sudden spikes inconsistent with recent activity. Compare results against typical baseline expectations and flag deviations exceeding thresholds (e.g., >90% sustained usage or <5% on all CPUs).
solution.algorithm: Parse command output to extract %idle or %user/%system values. Apply threshold-based anomaly detection logic.
